critic_raw:
This experiment tests a CNN on echocardiogram sequences versus multivariate linear regression on tabular clinical features for predicting a continuous 12-month heart disease risk score.

The ground truth label is a risk score at 12-month follow-up constructed from 'standardized measures including ejection fraction, left ventricular mass index, diastolic dysfunction grade, and validated prognostic indices.' These components are themselves measured by echocardiogram at follow-up. If ejection fraction at follow-up is part of the label, and ejection fraction is also visible in the baseline echocardiogram images the CNN is trained on, then the CNN is learning to predict a future echocardiographic measurement from a past echocardiographic measurement of the same quantity. This is not learning a risk score from image features — it is learning image-to-image regression where baseline and follow-up measurements are correlated through structural cardiac disease. The baseline (tabular regression) cannot exploit this because it uses demographic/clinical data at baseline only. The comparison is between two fundamentally different tasks being solved by the two models, not the same task.

Model selection uses 'retraining on combined train+validation data after hyperparameters are locked.' This is appropriate for the final model, but the evaluation on the test set is then performed on a model trained on strictly more data than the model whose hyperparameters were selected. If the combined train+validation data shifts the effective distribution seen during training (e.g., by including patients from year 4 not seen in initial training), the test performance may not reflect the hyperparameter-tuned performance on validation, making model selection potentially unreliable.

Site-conditional batch normalization is described as a technique for handling multi-site imaging protocol variation. Batch normalization statistics are computed per-batch during training. With multiple sites and variable site frequencies, the batch statistics will be dominated by high-volume sites, causing the 'site-conditional' component to be effective only for large sites and providing minimal correction for rare sites that may be overrepresented in test data. This is a known failure mode of site-conditioning via batch norm that requires specific per-site batch composition strategies not described here.

The CNN is pre-trained on ImageNet and fine-tuned end-to-end. ImageNet pre-training provides 2D RGB image features. Echocardiograms are grayscale, time-varying sequences with domain-specific features (speckle noise, anatomical landmarks, cardiac motion). Transfer from ImageNet introduces inductive biases (texture detectors, color edge detectors) that are irrelevant or harmful for ultrasound images. Domain-appropriate pre-training (e.g., on MIMIC-Echo or equivalent) would be more appropriate; the use of ImageNet pre-training is presented as standard practice without acknowledging this mismatch.
all_issues_raised: The 12-month follow-up risk score includes echocardiographic measurements (ejection fraction, LV mass index) that are also present in the baseline echocardiogram images used as CNN input. The CNN is thus learning image-to-image regression of the same structural measurements over time, not risk prediction from novel image features, which is a fundamentally easier task than the baseline model is solving.

Retraining on combined train+validation data after hyperparameter selection means the final evaluated model was trained on different data than the model used for hyperparameter selection, making the validation performance estimate not directly representative of the final model's tuning.

Site-conditional batch normalization is dominated by high-volume sites during batch statistics computation; rare sites receive inadequate normalization correction, creating a silent failure mode for sites underrepresented in training batches.

ImageNet pre-training introduces 2D RGB texture and color edge detectors as inductive biases that are irrelevant for grayscale ultrasound sequences with speckle noise and cardiac motion. Domain-appropriate pre-training is not considered.
all_issues_adjudicated: The 12-month follow-up risk score includes echocardiographic measurements (ejection fraction, LV mass index) that are also present in the baseline echocardiogram images used as CNN input. The CNN is thus learning image-to-image regression of the same structural measurements over time, not risk prediction from novel image features, which is a fundamentally easier task than the baseline model is solving.

Retraining on combined train+validation data after hyperparameter selection means the final evaluated model was trained on different data than the model used for hyperparameter selection, making the validation performance estimate not directly representative of the final model's tuning.

Site-conditional batch normalization is dominated by high-volume sites during batch statistics computation; rare sites receive inadequate normalization correction, creating a silent failure mode for sites underrepresented in training batches.

ImageNet pre-training introduces 2D RGB texture and color edge detectors as inductive biases that are irrelevant for grayscale ultrasound sequences with speckle noise and cardiac motion. Domain-appropriate pre-training is not considered.
